front: Work: the failing marriage of Kaname and Misako, who share a disdain for 'Osaka traits'
Work: Kaname attends bunraku performances and realizes the Eternal Woman is embodied by the doll of Koharu in Love Suicides at Sonezaki
Work: O-Hisa's archaic teeth-blackening is found 'filthy and barbarous' by Misako in this novel
Work: Kaname skims an unexpurgated Arabian Nights and sleeps with the half-Korean, half-Russian prostitute Louise
Work: a proud Osaka family whose fortunes have declined; Taeko has a scandalous fling with the lower-class Itakura
Work: Yukiko resists getting married; Sachiko disapproves of Taeko's affair
Work: The Maids retells this Tanizaki novel from the servants' point of view
Work: Joji trains a 15-year-old waitress reminiscent of Mary Pickford to be Western, but she ends up dominating him
Work: Joji idolizes Mary Pickford and his dance instructor Madam Shlemskaya in this novel
Work: this novel consists of alternating diaries by a wife and her husband, who tries to spark an affair between her and their friend Kimura
Work: this essay discusses the beauty of toilets as a place of 'spiritual repose' and contrasts light and dark in Japanese aesthetics
Author: in this author's novella The Secret History of the Lord of Musashi, the protagonist places a servant in a hole to simulate decapitation and orders his wife to cut off the servant's nose
Author: this author wrote Quicksand about the love affair between Sonoko Kakiuchi and art student Mitsuko
Author: this author's last published work, Diary of a Mad Old Man, follows Utsugi's attraction to his daughter-in-law Satsuko
Author: in this author's The Devils in Daylight, Sonomura cracks a code based on Poe's Gold-Bug and uses peepholes to observe a murder
